meta.subject: Religious Education
meta.philosophy: Quality lifelong education for all which is accessible, inclusive and relevant to individual, national and global needs and value systems. The syllabus aims to make learning at Ordinary Secondary School level more meaningful and enjoyable as it is highly activity oriented and allows for a smooth transition from Ordinary Secondary School Level to the Advanced Secondary School level or Tertiary Education.
meta.competence_learning: The Religious Education Syllabus for Ordinary Level Secondary Education has been reviewed to provide a national age-appropriate curriculum for learners from Form 1 to 4. The syllabus aims to provide quality education that is aligned with the Competence Based Curriculum and twenty first Century Skills. It also aims to impart the knowledge, skills, values and positive attitudes that are aimed at enabling learners to live and grow into productive and useful members of their communities and the Zambian society.
goals: To impart the knowledge, skills, values and positive attitudes that enable learners to live and grow into productive and useful members of their communities and the Zambian society.
To facilitate the holistic development of learners' physical, mental, emotional, social, spiritual and moral faculties.
To foster understanding, appreciation and respect for cultural and other aspects of plurality in Zambian society.
To help learners understand the belief systems of others, clarify their own belief systems, deal with problems and resolve conflicts.
To promote and encourage the cross-pollination of ideas among learners of different ethnic backgrounds.
To help learners develop intellectually and seek to refine their critical thinking skills, judgements and acquisition of knowledge.

subtopics: Division and hatred in the four religions
specific_outcomes: Practise unity and love in daily living
Recognise other people's beliefs and practices
Apply religious teachings on overcoming division and hatred
Relate religious teachings on sin, forgiveness and reconciliation to real life situations

subtopics: The teachings of the four religions in Zambia on freedom in community
specific_outcomes: Demonstrate understanding of the importance of freedom in community
Practice teamwork in the community
Apply teachings of the four religions in Zambia on freedom in community
Analyse personal strength and weakness
Practise Social and Emotional Learning competences
Apply teachings of the four religions in Zambia on self-esteem

subtopics: Religious teachings on Ambitions and Hope
specific_outcomes: Promote one's ambitions
Demonstrate creativity and innovation in ambitions and hope
Apply hope in daily life
Analyse religious teachings on ambitions and hope

subtopics: Religious teachings on Justice in society
specific_outcomes: Demonstrate understanding of justice in society
Demonstrate how good governance promotes social justice
Apply religious teachings on Justice in society
Recognise significance of work in society
Use work as a source of investment
Show that entrepreneurial activities constitute work
Show positive attitudes towards work
Analyse teachings of the four religions on work
